meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 6)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Identify and correct plural noun errors in sentences.

questions.question: What is the plural of "boy"?
questions.explanation: boy → boys. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: The plural of "brush" is "brushes".
questions.explanation: Yes! brush ends in -sh, so we add -es: brush → brushes.

questions.question: Write the plural of "boy".
questions.explanation: boy → boys. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Six ___ are on the rocks. (bug)
questions.explanation: "Six" tells us we need a plural. bug → bugs.

questions.question: "Cats" and "dogs" are both plural nouns.
questions.explanation: Yes! Both cat → cats and dog → dogs are plural forms.

questions.question: Write the plural of "fox".
questions.explanation: fox → foxes. Words ending in -x add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence has the correct plural noun?
options: Four girl dance to the music.
Four girls dance to the music.
Four girles dance to the music.
Four girlies dance to the music.
questions.explanation: girl → girls. "Four" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: brush/bug/ruler = one (singular). boys/foxes = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Ana sees some birds on two branches."

questions.question: The plural of "kite" is "kites".
questions.explanation: Yes! kite → kites. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Write the plural of "house".
questions.explanation: house → houses. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which noun adds -es because it ends in -x?
questions.explanation: fox ends in -x, so fox → foxes (add -es).

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -sh or -x add -es.

questions.question: In the sentence "There are many wishes," the noun "wishes" is plural.
questions.explanation: Yes! "many wishes" — wishes is the plural of wish.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Three houses are near the roads."

questions.question: Write the plural of "bus".
questions.explanation: bus → buses. Words ending in -s add -es.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: We see many church in the city.
We see many churches in the city.
We see many churchs in the city.
We see many churchies in the city.
questions.explanation: church → churches (add -es). "many" tells us we need a plural.

questions.question: Which group shows the -es rule correctly applied?
questions.explanation: box ends in -x, so box → boxes (add -es). Only this one is correct.

questions.question: In the sentence "My friend and I have two bags," the noun "bags" is singular.
questions.explanation: "two bags" means more than one bag — that's plural, not singular.

questions.question: Write the plural of "shell".
questions.explanation: shell → shells. Regular nouns add -s.
